title: How to File ITR Online
meta_description: Step-by-step guide to filing Income Tax Return online in India. Which ITR form to use, documents needed, deadlines, and common mistakes to avoid.

hero.one_liner: File your income tax return online in under 30 minutes — step-by-step guide for salaried individuals and freelancers

sections.heading: Who Needs to File ITR?
content: You must file an Income Tax Return if your gross total income before deductions exceeds ₹3 lakh (under new tax regime) or ₹2.5 lakh (under old regime) in a financial year. Even if your taxable income is zero after deductions, filing ITR is recommended — you need it for visa applications, loan approvals, and to carry forward losses.

If you are salaried and your employer deducts TDS, filing ITR helps you claim refunds for excess tax paid, and declare additional income or deductions your employer doesn't know about (like rental income, capital gains from stocks, or 80C deductions beyond what's in Form 16).

rows.label: Form 16
rows.value: From your employer — shows salary, TDS deducted, and tax computation

rows.label: Form 26AS / AIS
rows.value: Download from incometax.gov.in — shows all TDS, TCS, and tax deposits

rows.label: Bank statements
rows.value: For interest income from savings accounts, FDs

rows.label: Investment proofs
rows.value: PPF, ELSS, LIC, NPS receipts for 80C/80CCD deductions

rows.label: Home loan certificate
rows.value: From bank — shows principal (80C) and interest (80EEA/24b) breakup

rows.label: Capital gains statement
rows.value: From broker (Zerodha, Groww) if you traded stocks/mutual funds

rows.label: PAN & Aadhaar
rows.value: Must be linked. Bank account details for refund credit

steps.title: Login to incometax.gov.in
steps.description: Go to the Income Tax e-Filing portal. Login with your PAN as user ID. If first time, register using PAN, Aadhaar, and mobile number.

steps.title: Select the correct ITR form
steps.description: ITR-1 (Sahaj) for salaried individuals with income up to ₹50 lakh from salary, one house property, and other sources. ITR-2 if you have capital gains or multiple house properties. ITR-3 if you have business/freelance income.

steps.title: Choose tax regime
steps.description: New regime (lower rates, fewer deductions) or Old regime (higher rates, allows 80C/80D deductions). The portal has a comparison tool to show which saves more tax for you.

steps.title: Fill income details
steps.description: Most data is pre-filled from Form 26AS and AIS. Verify salary, TDS, bank interest, and other income. Add any missing income sources.

steps.title: Add deductions (Old regime only)
steps.description: Section 80C (PPF, ELSS, LIC — up to ₹1.5L), 80D (health insurance — up to ₹25K/₹50K), 80CCD(1B) (NPS — additional ₹50K), HRA, home loan interest.

steps.title: Verify tax computation and submit
steps.description: Review the computed tax. If you owe tax, pay via Challan 280 before submitting. If refund is due, confirm your bank account details.

steps.title: e-Verify your ITR
steps.description: Within 30 days of filing, you must verify. Easiest method: Aadhaar OTP. Other options: net banking, DSC, or sending signed ITR-V to CPC Bengaluru.

faqs.question: What is the deadline for filing ITR?
faqs.answer: July 31 of the assessment year for individuals (e.g., for FY 2025-26 income, file by July 31, 2026). Late filing is allowed until December 31 with a penalty of ₹5,000 (₹1,000 if income is below ₹5 lakh).

faqs.question: Which ITR form should salaried people use?
faqs.answer: ITR-1 (Sahaj) if your total income is under ₹50 lakh and comes from salary, one house property, and interest/other sources. If you have capital gains from stocks or mutual funds, use ITR-2.

faqs.question: Old regime or new regime — which is better?
faqs.answer: It depends on your deductions. If you claim more than ₹3-3.5 lakh in deductions (80C, 80D, HRA, home loan), old regime usually saves more. If you have few deductions, new regime is better. Use the comparison tool on the IT portal.

faqs.question: What if I miss the deadline?
faqs.answer: You can file a belated return until December 31 with a ₹5,000 late fee. After that, you can only file an updated return (ITR-U) within 2 years, but you'll pay additional 25-50% tax on any dues.

faqs.question: Do I need a CA to file ITR?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily. If you're salaried with straightforward income, the e-filing portal is designed for self-filing. Use ITR-1, and most data is pre-filled. You might need a CA only if you have business income, complex capital gains, or foreign income.

disclaimer: This article is for general information only. Tax laws change frequently. Verify current rules on incometax.gov.in and consult a qualified CA for personalized tax advice.
